entries.title: LETTER 33 on judges 19:24
entries.excerpt: When he saw that he was making little headway, he added that he had a maiden daughter and he offered her to them, with great sorrow, since he was her parent, but with less damage to the favor he owed his guest. He considered a public crime more tolerable than private disgrace.

entries.title: On the Duties of the Clergy 3.19.114 on judges 19:24
entries.excerpt: Then the old man offered these wicked men his daughter, a virgin, and the concubine with whom she shared her bed, only that violence might not be inflicted on his guest.
